cacheaudit.notes: x86-32 only; no speculative execution modeling; quantitative upper bounds

spectector.notes: x86-64; speculative constant-time (yes/no); no quantitative bounds

binsecrel.notes: x86-64; relational symbolic execution; bounded path enumeration; no composition

benchmarks.name: aes-128-ecb
benchmarks.description: AES-128 ECB encryption: T-table implementation from OpenSSL 1.1.1w, key schedule + single block encrypt

cacheaudit.notes: x86-32 recompilation required; no speculative model; looser bound due to monolithic analysis

spectector.notes: Reports speculative-constant-time violation (binary yes/no); no quantitative bound

binsecrel.notes: Bounded to 500 paths; closer bound but higher resource usage; no composition

benchmarks.name: aes-256-gcm
benchmarks.description: AES-256-GCM authenticated encryption: AES-NI encrypt + CLMUL-based GHASH, from BoringSSL

cacheaudit.notes: Cannot analyze: requires AES-NI and CLMUL intrinsics not supported on x86-32

spectector.notes: Reports SECURE (speculative-constant-time holds for AES-NI path)

binsecrel.notes: Confirms zero leakage but with 20x resource overhead vs LeakCert

benchmarks.name: chacha20-poly1305
benchmarks.description: ChaCha20-Poly1305 AEAD: ARX stream cipher + Poly1305 MAC, from libsodium 1.0.19

cacheaudit.notes: x86-32 recompile; correctly identifies zero leakage (ARX is inherently constant-time)

spectector.notes: SECURE; ChaCha20 ARX operations are speculative-constant-time

binsecrel.notes: Confirms zero leakage; consistent across all tools

benchmarks.name: rsa-2048
benchmarks.description: RSA-2048 private key operation: CRT with Montgomery multiplication, constant-time from BoringSSL

cacheaudit.notes: Timeout after 3600s; Montgomery ladder too complex for monolithic abstract interpretation on x86-32

spectector.notes: Reports speculative violation in branch predictor interaction; no quantification of severity

binsecrel.notes: Timeout after 3600s; path explosion in modular exponentiation loop

benchmarks.name: ecdsa-p256
benchmarks.description: ECDSA P-256 sign: constant-time scalar multiplication via Montgomery ladder, from BoringSSL

cacheaudit.notes: Cannot analyze: P-256 field arithmetic uses 64-bit limbs, incompatible with x86-32

spectector.notes: Reports INSECURE; identifies speculative store bypass but cannot quantify

binsecrel.notes: Completes with aggressive path merging; bound is 29% looser than LeakCert

benchmarks.name: sha-256
benchmarks.description: SHA-256 single block compression: reference C implementation from NIST CAVP suite

cacheaudit.notes: Correctly identifies zero leakage; simple control flow, no secret-dependent branches

binsecrel.notes: Confirms zero leakage; all tools agree on this benchmark

benchmarks.name: x25519
benchmarks.description: X25519 Diffie-Hellman: constant-time Montgomery ladder over Curve25519, from libsodium 1.0.19

cacheaudit.notes: x86-32 recompile changes limb structure; false positive from 32-bit field arithmetic adaptation

spectector.notes: SECURE; Montgomery ladder is speculative-constant-time in libsodium

binsecrel.notes: Confirms zero leakage on native x86-64; agrees with LeakCert

benchmarks.name: hkdf-sha256
benchmarks.description: HKDF-SHA256 extract + expand: composed from HMAC-SHA256, tests compositional analysis

cacheaudit.notes: Zero leakage confirmed; but cannot verify composition — analyzes HKDF monolithically

spectector.notes: SECURE; HMAC and SHA-256 are individually constant-time

binsecrel.notes: Confirms zero leakage; monolithic analysis only

benchmarks.name: boringssl-full
benchmarks.description: Full BoringSSL crypto library: 247 exported functions, tests scalability of compositional analysis

leakcert.notes: Completed full-library analysis in 43 minutes; 7 violations across T-table AES, RSA, and ECDSA paths

cacheaudit.notes: Cannot analyze: x86-32 only, library too large for monolithic analysis; no composition support

spectector.notes: Timeout after 3600s on full library; designed for single-function analysis

binsecrel.notes: Timeout after 3600s; path explosion renders full-library analysis infeasible

cacheaudit.notes: x86-32 limitation excludes 3 benchmarks; timeout on RSA-2048; no speculative modeling

spectector.notes: Provides only binary yes/no for speculative constant-time; no quantitative bounds; library-level timeout

binsecrel.notes: Closest competitor on quantitative bounds; 1.2-1.3x looser bounds where both complete; no composition; higher memory

comparison_highlights.precision_advantage: LeakCert bounds are within 1.6x of ground truth; Binsec/Rel is 1.9x where both complete
comparison_highlights.scalability_advantage: LeakCert is the only tool that completes full-library analysis (BoringSSL, 247 functions, 43 min)
comparison_highlights.speculative_advantage: LeakCert provides quantitative speculative leakage bounds; Spectector gives only binary verdict
comparison_highlights.composition_advantage: LeakCert generates reusable contracts with avg 10.3% overhead; no baseline supports composition
comparison_highlights.coverage: LeakCert completes 9/9 benchmarks; CacheAudit 5/9; Spectector 8/9; Binsec/Rel 7/9

cve_regression_tests.description: CacheBleed: cache-bank conflict leakage in OpenSSL RSA
ROHNP: return-address side channel in ECDSA nonce generation
ChaCha20-Poly1305 nonce reuse leakage in OpenSSL
CrossTalk: speculative data sampling across cores (SRBDS)
